syntax_13733.creator: F0cus
syntax_13733.title: File properties
syntax_13733.description: You can get file name, path, size and content

en: You can get file name, path, size and content
Loops over json entries/keys/indices
will return last http response

syntax_13475.creator: F0cus
syntax_13475.title: Check if JSON file exists
syntax_13475.syntax_pattern:
[skjson] json file %string% exists
[skjson] json file %string% does(n't| not) exist

syntax_13490.creator: F0cus
syntax_13490.title: Formatting skript variable to json
syntax_13490.description: Formats the skript variable to json. This is used to format the skript variable to json.

en: Formats the skript variable to json. This is used to format the skript variable to json.

syntax_13476.creator: F0cus
syntax_13476.title: Check if JSON is empty
syntax_13476.syntax_pattern:
[skjson] json %jsonelement% is empty
[skjson] json %jsonelement% is(n't| not) empty

syntax_13477.creator: F0cus
syntax_13477.title: Is JSON file cached
syntax_13477.syntax_pattern:
[skjson] [the] json storage [with [the] id] %string% is cached
[skjson] [the] json storage [with [the] id] %string% is(n't| not) cached

syntax_13478.creator: F0cus
syntax_13478.title: JSON file is listened by storage watcher
syntax_13478.syntax_pattern:
[skjson] [the] json storage [with [the] id] %string% is listened
[skjson] [the] json storage [with [the] id] %string% is(n't| not) listened

syntax_13496.creator: F0cus
syntax_13496.title: Json values
syntax_13496.description:
Returns the value of the json at the given path. If the path is not provided, it will return all values from the json.
If the path is a number, it will return the value at that index in the array.
If the path is a string, it will return the value at that key in the object.
If the path is empty, it will return all values from the json.

en:
Returns the value of the json at the given path. If the path is not provided, it will return all values from the json.
If the path is a number, it will return the value at that index in the array.
If the path is a string, it will return the value at that key in the object.
If the path is empty, it will return all values from the json.

syntax_13486.creator: F0cus
syntax_13486.title: Unbind the storage for the given id
syntax_13486.description: Unbinds the storage for the given id. This is used to unbind the storage from the file.

en: Unbinds the storage for the given id. This is used to unbind the storage from the file.

syntax_13481.creator: F0cus
syntax_13481.title: Bind JSON file as simply string id
syntax_13481.description: Allows you create an simply string if for bound json file
syntax_13481.syntax_pattern:
[skjson] (bind|link) json file %string% as %string%
[skjson] (bind|link) json file %string% as %string% and let bind storage watcher

en: Allows you create an simply string if for bound json file

syntax_13484.creator: F0cus
syntax_13484.title: Save cached json to file
syntax_13484.description: Saves the cached json to a file. This is used to save the cache to a file.
syntax_13484.syntax_pattern:
[skjson] save json storage [id] %string%
[skjson] save all json storages

en: Saves the cached json to a file. This is used to save the cache to a file.

syntax_13485.creator: F0cus
syntax_13485.title: Send created/prepared request
syntax_13485.description:
Sends a previously created HTTP request to the specified URL
The request must be created using request creation commands (e.g. 'create request').
Supports both synchronous and asynchronous sending using the 'non' or 'not' tag.

en:
Sends a previously created HTTP request to the specified URL
The request must be created using request creation commands (e.g. 'create request').
Supports both synchronous and asynchronous sending using the 'non' or 'not' tag.

syntax_13487.creator: F0cus
syntax_13487.title: Virtual json storage
syntax_13487.description: Creates a virtual json storage. This is used to create a virtual json storage that can be used to store data in memory.

en: Creates a virtual json storage. This is used to create a virtual json storage that can be used to store data in memory.

syntax_13488.creator: F0cus
syntax_13488.title: formatting Json to Skript list variable
syntax_13488.description: Maps the json to a skript list variable. This is used to map the json to a skript list variable.
syntax_13488.compatible_addon_version: 1.9, 2.9 - Support mapping json from functions, 4.1 performance increase

en: Maps the json to a skript list variable. This is used to map the json to a skript list variable.

syntax_13489.creator: F0cus
syntax_13489.title: Count keys/values in the json
syntax_13489.description: Counts the number of keys or values in the json. This is used to count the number of keys or values in the json.

en: Counts the number of keys or values in the json. This is used to count the number of keys or values in the json.

syntax_13474.creator: F0cus
syntax_13474.title: Json watcher save
syntax_13474.description: will only run when the json watcher notices a change in the file

en: will only run when the json watcher notices a change in the file

syntax_13492.creator: F0cus
syntax_13492.title: Get all files paths in given directory
syntax_13492.description: Returns all json files from the given directory. The directory must be a valid path.

en: Returns all json files from the given directory. The directory must be a valid path.

syntax_13494.creator: F0cus
syntax_13494.title: Get cached json storage
syntax_13494.description: Get cached json storage. This is used to get the cached json storage. If the id is not provided, it will return all json storages.
syntax_13494.syntax_pattern:
[skjson] json storage of id %string%
[skjson] all json storages

en: Get cached json storage. This is used to get the cached json storage. If the id is not provided, it will return all json storages.

syntax_13495.creator: F0cus
syntax_13495.title: Json loop
syntax_13495.description:
That will allow loop through json, and get key/index or value
**json-value**, **json-key**

en:
That will allow loop through json, and get key/index or value
**json-value**, **json-key**

syntax_13479.creator: F0cus
syntax_13479.title: Json has value/keys
syntax_13479.description: You can check if the inserted keys or values already in your specified json

en: You can check if the inserted keys or values already in your specified json

syntax_13480.creator: F0cus
syntax_13480.title: Type of Json
syntax_13480.description: You can get type of given Json.
syntax_13480.syntax_pattern:
[skjson] json type of %jsonelement% is (json[ ](object)|json[ ](array)|json[ ](primitive)|json[ ](null))
[skjson] json type of %jsonelement% (is(n't| not)) (json[ ](object)|json[ ](array)|json[ ](primitive)|json[ ](null))

en: You can get type of given Json.

syntax_13493.creator: F0cus
syntax_13493.title: Get all keys from Json object
syntax_13493.description: Returns all keys from the given json object. If the path is provided, it will return all keys from the object at that path.

en: Returns all keys from the given json object. If the path is provided, it will return all keys from the object at that path.

syntax_13506.creator: F0cus
syntax_13506.title: Response of http request [non blocking]
syntax_13506.description:
Represent response of http request
You can get those values (status, status code, body, headers)

en:
Represent response of http request
You can get those values (status, status code, body, headers)
Get last response from request

syntax_13511.creator: F0cus
syntax_13511.title: Size of json object/array
syntax_13511.description:
Returns the size of the json object or array.
If the json is null or primitive, it will return null.
If the json is an object, it will return the number of keys.
If the json is an array, it will return the number of elements.

en:
Returns the size of the json object or array.
If the json is null or primitive, it will return null.
If the json is an object, it will return the number of keys.
If the json is an array, it will return the number of elements.

syntax_13514.creator: F0cus
syntax_13514.title: Watcher event value-expression UUID
syntax_13514.description:
value-expression for getting uuid from current watcher event
Returns the uuid of the watcher

en:
value-expression for getting uuid from current watcher event
Returns the uuid of the watcher

syntax_13515.creator: F0cus
syntax_13515.title: Watcher event value-expression json content
syntax_13515.description:
value-expression for getting json content from current watcher event
Returns json element (content) of the watched|changed file

en:
value-expression for getting json content from current watcher event
Returns json element (content) of the watched|changed file
Json element representation
Representation instance of Response

syntax_13520.creator: F0cus
syntax_13520.title: attachment
syntax_13520.description: Create new Attachment for the web request from path to file, when the file starts with */ the file will be found automatically.

en: Create new Attachment for the web request from path to file, when the file starts with */ the file will be found automatically.

syntax_13499.creator: F0cus
syntax_13499.title: Pretty print
syntax_13499.description: Allows you print out the json with formatted format, 4 tab padding and colored for some elements
syntax_13499.syntax_pattern:
[skjson] %jsonelement% as pretty[ printed]
[skjson] %jsonelement% as uncolo[u]red pretty[ printed]

en: Allows you print out the json with formatted format, 4 tab padding and colored for some elements

syntax_13512.creator: F0cus
syntax_13512.title: Support literals
syntax_13512.description: Returns the first, second, third, last, random or custom value of the json. This is used to get the first, second, third, last, random or custom value of the json.
syntax_13512.syntax_pattern:
[skjson] (1st|first) (value|key) of %jsonelement%
[skjson] (2nd|second) (value|key) of %jsonelement%
[skjson] (3rd|third) (value|key) of %jsonelement%
[skjson] last (value|key) of %jsonelement%
[skjson] random (value|key) of %jsonelement%
[skjson] %integer%. (value|key) of %jsonelement%

en: Returns the first, second, third, last, random or custom value of the json. This is used to get the first, second, third, last, random or custom value of the json.

syntax_13500.creator: F0cus
syntax_13500.title: Request attachment/s
syntax_13500.description: set/add/reset or get the current request attachment

en: set/add/reset or get the current request attachment

syntax_13503.creator: F0cus
syntax_13503.title: Request query params
syntax_13503.description: set/add/reset or get the current request query params

en: set/add/reset or get the current request query params

syntax_13508.creator: F0cus
syntax_13508.title: Response status
syntax_13508.description:
Receive response status
That could be OK, UNKNOWN or FAILED

en:
Receive response status
That could be OK, UNKNOWN or FAILED

syntax_13509.creator: F0cus
syntax_13509.title: Response status code
syntax_13509.description:
Receive response status code
That could be anything from 100 to 504

en:
Receive response status code
That could be anything from 100 to 504
Representation instance of Request

syntax_13513.creator: F0cus
syntax_13513.title: Watcher event value-expression File
syntax_13513.description:
value-expression for getting file/link from current watcher event
Returns path of changed|watched file

en:
value-expression for getting file/link from current watcher event
Returns path of changed|watched file

syntax_13482.creator: F0cus
syntax_13482.title: Cached storage watcher - un/bind from file
syntax_13482.description: Binds or unbinds a storage watcher to a file. This is used to watch for changes in the file and update the cache accordingly.
syntax_13482.syntax_pattern:
[skjson] bind storage watcher to %string%
[skjson] unbind storage watcher from %string%

en: Binds or unbinds a storage watcher to a file. This is used to watch for changes in the file and update the cache accordingly.

syntax_13483.creator: F0cus
syntax_13483.title: Create JSON file with/out content
syntax_13483.description:
Creates a JSON file with the given name and content. If the file already exists, it will be replaced if the configuration is set to replace=true.
If the content is not provided, an empty JSON object will be created.
The configuration can be used to set the encoding and other options.
syntax_13483.syntax_pattern:
[skjson] new json file %string%
[skjson] new json file %string% with [content] %objects%
[skjson] create json file %string% [with configuration<\[\s*((\w+)=([\w-]+)(?:,\s*)?)+\s*\]>]
[skjson] create json file %string% and write to it %object% [with configuration<\[\s*((\w+)=([\w-]+)(?:,\s*)?)+\s*\]>]

en:
Creates a JSON file with the given name and content. If the file already exists, it will be replaced if the configuration is set to replace=true.
If the content is not provided, an empty JSON object will be created.
The configuration can be used to set the encoding and other options.

syntax_13497.creator: F0cus
syntax_13497.title: New json element
syntax_13497.description:
Creates a new json element from any object, file or website.
This is used to create a new json element from any object, file or website.
syntax_13497.syntax_pattern:
[skjson] json from file %strings%
[skjson] json from website %strings%
[skjson] json (from|of) %objects%

en:
Creates a new json element from any object, file or website.
This is used to create a new json element from any object, file or website.

syntax_13498.creator: F0cus
syntax_13498.title: Prepare Web requests
syntax_13498.description:
allowed methods are [GET, POST, PUT, HEAD, MOCK, DELETE, PATCH]
allowed value type of content is Json or stringify json (Json as String) e.g. "{""Test"": true}"
allowed value type of header is Json or (Pairs e.g. "Content-Type: application/Json", "Allow: restrict")

You can execute the request by 'send prepared {_request}', otherwise the request will be not sent, but the request will be still stored
And you can get response status/content/headers like in the examples

en:
allowed methods are [GET, POST, PUT, HEAD, MOCK, DELETE, PATCH]
allowed value type of content is Json or stringify json (Json as String) e.g. "{""Test"": true}"
allowed value type of header is Json or (Pairs e.g. "Content-Type: application/Json", "Allow: restrict")

You can execute the request by 'send prepared {_request}', otherwise the request will be not sent, but the request will be still stored
And you can get response status/content/headers like in the examples

syntax_13713.creator: F0cus
syntax_13713.title: body of request/response
syntax_13713.description:
set/reset or get the current request body
get response body

en:
set/reset or get the current request body
get response body

syntax_13714.creator: F0cus
syntax_13714.title: headers of request/response
syntax_13714.description:
set or get the current request headers
get response headers

en:
set or get the current request headers
get response headers

syntax_13735.creator: F0cus
syntax_13735.title: Request follow redirects
syntax_13735.description: set/get or set requests configuration for follow redirects

en: set/get or set requests configuration for follow redirects

syntax_13736.creator: F0cus
syntax_13736.title: Request http version
syntax_13736.description: set/get or reset the current http version of request

en: set/get or reset the current http version of request

syntax_13737.creator: F0cus
syntax_13737.title: Request timeout
syntax_13737.description: set/get or reset the current timeout of request

en: set/get or reset the current timeout of request

syntax_13734.creator: F0cus
syntax_13734.title: JSON
syntax_13734.description: Gets, sets, removes values/keys etc.. of JSON

en: Gets, sets, removes values/keys etc.. of JSON

Changer.
You can SET/ADD/REMOVE/RESET/DELETE/REMOVE_ALL

syntax_13729.creator: F0cus
syntax_13729.title: Sort JSON in descending/ascending order by key/value
syntax_13729.description:
Sorts the JSON element in descending/ascending order by key/value.
The JSON element can be a JSON object or an array.
The sort type can be specified as a string in the format of 'by key ascending' or 'by value descending'.
The sort type can also be specified as a SortType enum value.

en:
Sorts the JSON element in descending/ascending order by key/value.
The JSON element can be a JSON object or an array.
The sort type can be specified as a string in the format of 'by key ascending' or 'by value descending'.
The sort type can also be specified as a SortType enum value.

syntax_13510.creator: F0cus
syntax_13510.title: Simple json literal
syntax_13510.description:
Explanatory notes
* - This expression is used to get a values (list) from a json object|array.
  - Need to be at the end of the path.


This syntax is a simplification for json path, and shortening the notation and getting closer to the form (jq or other popular tools)
**RECOMMENDATION**: Use this syntax exclusively for paths that are a maximum of 2 keys deep. - for Creating
- Use rather value/key path expression
Can handle strict (get/set/remove)
all variables or expressions must be in “% ... %”
This syntax is strictly limited to its intended use; it does allow other expressions or variables to be used.
**RECOMMENDATION**: Use this syntax exclusively for paths that are a maximum of 2 keys deep.
Can handle strict (get/set/remove)
* at the end means you want to return a skript list.

en:
Explanatory notes
* - This expression is used to get a values (list) from a json object|array.
  - Need to be at the end of the path.


This syntax is a simplification for json path, and shortening the notation and getting closer to the form (jq or other popular tools)
**RECOMMENDATION**: Use this syntax exclusively for paths that are a maximum of 2 keys deep. - for Creating
- Use rather value/key path expression
Can handle strict (get/set/remove)
all variables or expressions must be in “% ... %”
This syntax is strictly limited to its intended use; it does allow other expressions or variables to be used.
**RECOMMENDATION**: Use this syntax exclusively for paths that are a maximum of 2 keys deep.
Can handle strict (get/set/remove)
* at the end means you want to return a skript list.
